title: लक्ष्मी अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मी अष्टकम् ॥

महालक्ष्म्यष्टकस्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिमान्नरः।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति राज्यं प्राप्नोति सर्वदा॥

नमस्तेऽस्तु महामाये श्रीपीठे सुरपूजिते।
शंखचक्रगदाहस्ते महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१॥

नमस्ते गरुडारूढे कोलासुरभयंकरि।
सर्वपापहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२॥

सर्वज्ञे सर्ववरदे सर्वदुष्टभयंकरि।
सर्वदुःखहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥३॥

सिद्धिबुद्धिप्रदे देवि भुक्तिमुक्तिप्रदायिनि।
मन्त्रमूर्ते सदा देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥४॥

आद्यन्तरहिते देवि आद्यशक्तिमहेश्वरि।
योगजे योगसम्भूते महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥५॥

स्थूलसूक्ष्ममहारौद्रे महाशक्तिमहोदरे।
महापापहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥६॥

पद्मासनस्थिते देवि परब्रह्मस्वरूपिणि।
परमेशि जगन्मातर्महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥७॥

श्वेताम्बरधरे देवि नानालंकारभूषिते।
जगत्स्थिते जगन्मातर्महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
महालक्ष्म्यष्टकं स्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिमान्नरः।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति राज्यं प्राप्नोति सर्वदा॥
एककाले पठेन्नित्यं महापापविनाशनम्।
द्विकालं यः पठेन्नित्यं धनधान्यसमन्वितम्॥
त्रिकालं यः पठेन्नित्यं महाशत्रुविनाशनम्।
महालक्ष्मीर्भवेन्नित्यं प्रसन्ना वरदा शुभा॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Ashtakam ||

Mahalakshmyashtakastotram yah pathed bhaktiman narah.
Sarvasiddhim avapnoti rajyam prapnoti sarvada.

Namaste'stu Mahamaye Shripithe Surapujite.
Shankha-chakra-gada-haste Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||1||

Namaste Garudarudhe Kolasura-bhayankari.
Sarva-papa-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||2||

Sarvajne Sarva-varade Sarva-dushta-bhayankari.
Sarva-duhkha-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||3||

Siddhi-buddhi-prade Devi Bhukti-mukti-pradayini.
Mantra-murte Sada Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||4||

Adyanta-rahite Devi Adya-shakti-maheshwari.
Yogaje Yoga-sambhute Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||5||

Sthula-sukshma-maha-raudre Maha-shakti-mahodare.
Maha-papa-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||6||

Padmasana-sthite Devi Parabrahma-swarupini.
Parameshi Jagan-matar Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||7||

Shvetambara-dhare Devi Nanalamkara-bhushite.
Jagat-sthite Jagan-matar Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||8||

|| Phalashruti ||
Mahalakshmyashtakam stotram yah pathed bhaktiman narah.
Sarvasiddhim avapnoti rajyam prapnoti sarvada.
Eka-kale pathen nityam maha-papa-vinashanam.
Dwi-kalam yah pathen nityam dhana-dhanya-samanvitam.
Tri-kalam yah pathen nityam maha-shatru-vinashanam.
Mahalakshmeer bhavet nityam prasanna varada shubha.
hindiMeaning:
हे महामाया! हे श्रीपीठ में स्थित देवताओं द्वारा पूजित! हे शंख, चक्र और गदा धारण करने वाली! हे महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे गरुड़ पर विराजमान! हे कोलासुर को भय देने वाली! हे सर्वपापहारिणी देवी! हे महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे सर्वज्ञ! हे सर्ववरदायिनी! हे सर्वदुष्टभयंकरी! हे सर्वदुःखहारिणी देवी! हे महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे सिद्धि और बुद्धि प्रदान करने वाली! हे भोग और मोक्ष देने वाली! हे मंत्रमूर्ति! हे देवी महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

जो भक्तिमान मनुष्य इस महालक्ष्मी अष्टक स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, वह सब सिद्धियों को प्राप्त करता है और सदा राज्य को प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of all eight types of wealth (ashta lakshmi)
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and negative karma
Invokes material prosperity and spiritual liberation
Traditionally recited for removal of enemies when recited three times daily
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and brings abundance
Pleases Goddess Mahalakshmi for her eternal grace
description: Lakshmi Ashtakam — लक्ष्मी अष्टकम् — is an eight-verse devotional hymn offered to Goddess Lakshmi. Attributed to Traditional, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove all sins and negative karma as well as remove poverty and bring abundance. Additionally, it is known to grant material prosperity and spiritual liberation. This prayer is traditionally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali to invoke divine grace. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Lakshmi Ashtakam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Ashtakam is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn to Goddess Mahalakshmi beginning Namastestu Mahamaye Shripithe Surapujite. It is traditionally attributed to Indra, the king of the devas, who composed it in praise of Mahalakshmi after she rescued the celestial realm from the demon Kolasura. For this reason it is also widely known as the Indra-krita Mahalakshmi Ashtakam. The text is preserved in the Padma Purana and circulates today in countless stotra collections, devotional CDs and printed pothis used during Diwali, Varalakshmi Vrata and weekly Lakshmi puja. (Sources: stotra-nidhi.com Mahalakshmi Ashtakam; Padma Purana stotra section; vedadhara.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Lakshmi Ashtakam the same text as Mahalakshmi Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the Lakshmi Ashtakam and the Mahalakshmi Ashtakam refer to the same eight-verse composition by Indra, with both names used interchangeably across publishers and digital sources. The text on this page is identical in its eight core verses (Namaste'stu Mahamaye through Shvetambara-dhare devi) to the Mahalakshmi Ashtakam published in Padma Purana editions, and only the title prefix Maha differs. The phalashruti — promising removal of sins by once-daily recitation, wealth and grain by twice-daily, and victory over enemies by thrice-daily — is also identical. Devotees therefore need not seek a separate Mahalakshmi Ashtakam when reciting Lakshmi Ashtakam. (Sources: Padma Purana stotra editions; comparative Sanskrit stotra collations.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the famous phalashruti promise about reciting it once, twice or three times a day?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti specifies a graduated reward structure: eka-kale pathen nityam maha-papa-vinashanam — once-a-day recitation destroys great sins; dwi-kalam yah pathen nityam dhana-dhanya-samanvitam — twice-daily recitation grants wealth and grain in abundance; tri-kalam yah pathen nityam maha-shatru-vinashanam — thrice-daily recitation destroys great enemies. Beyond these graded benefits, the opening phalashruti verse promises sarva-siddhi (all spiritual accomplishments) and rajyam (sovereignty/leadership), making it a comprehensive prayer for both worldly and spiritual fulfilment. Many devotees adopt the morning recitation as a permanent daily practice and add the noon and evening recitations during Lakshmi-paksha, Diwali week, and Varalakshmi Vrata. (Sources: traditional phalashruti commentary; drikpanchang.com Lakshmi puja vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Kolasura mentioned in the second verse of the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Kolasura is a demon described in the Padma Purana whom Mahalakshmi slew while seated on her vahana Garuda. The verse Namaste Garudarudhe Kolasura-bhayankari salutes her in this victorious aspect — mounted on Garuda, terror to Kolasura, remover of all sins. The episode is part of the larger Puranic motif in which Mahalakshmi takes a fierce form to protect the devas, parallel to Durga's slaying of Mahishasura. The reference distinguishes this Lakshmi Ashtakam from purely benedictory Lakshmi prayers by acknowledging the Goddess's protective and warlike aspect alongside her bestowal of wealth. (Sources: Padma Purana Lakshmi-mahatmya; Indra-stuti context.)

seoFaqs.question: When should one recite Lakshmi Ashtakam for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Lakshmi Ashtakam is most powerfully recited on Friday (Shukravar), the day sacred to Goddess Lakshmi and her planet Shukra, during Brahma muhurta before sunrise or at the time of evening lamp-lighting. Special occasions include Diwali (Lakshmi Puja night on Kartik Amavasya), Dhanteras, Varalakshmi Vrata (Shravan Shukla Friday before Purnima), Akshaya Tritiya, Margashirsha Lakshmivara Vrata, and the entire month of Margashirsha. Many businesses and households open their account books, jewellery and money safes during the recitation to invoke Lakshmi's grace on their wealth. (Sources: drikpanchang.com Diwali calendar; Vedic auspicious time guides.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Lakshmi Ashtakam compare with other Lakshmi stotras like Sri Suktam and Kanakadhara?
seoFaqs.answer: Lakshmi Ashtakam is the shortest and most popular of the major Lakshmi stotras, taking under five minutes to recite. The Sri Suktam from the Rig Veda Khila is a longer Vedic mantra-stuti of fifteen rik used in elaborate yajna and abhisheka rituals. The Kanakadhara Stotram by Adi Shankaracharya is a longer Sanskrit composition of eighteen to twenty-two verses traditionally recited to remove poverty (Shankaracharya famously caused a shower of golden amla fruits with it). The Lakshmi Hridayam from the Atharva Veda Khila Sukta is the most esoteric and is recited for the most intimate Lakshmi-darshan. Devotees often combine Lakshmi Ashtakam (daily core) with Sri Suktam (Vedic depth), Kanakadhara (poverty removal) and Lakshmi Hridayam (heart-essence) for a complete Lakshmi sadhana. (Sources: Adi Shankara stotra collections; Rig Veda Khila Sri Suktam; Atharva Veda Khila tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of bhukti-mukti-pradayini in verse 4?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 4 (Siddhi-buddhi-prade devi bhukti-mukti-pradayini) salutes Mahalakshmi as the bestower of both bhukti — material enjoyment and worldly prosperity — and mukti — spiritual liberation. This dual capacity is the unique theological signature of Mahalakshmi: unlike many devis who are sought primarily for one or the other, Lakshmi is invoked as the giver of all four purusharthas — dharma, artha, kama and moksha. The compound word also salutes her as siddhi-buddhi-prade — granter of supernatural attainments and discriminating intelligence — and mantra-murte, the embodied form of the sacred mantra. This verse anchors the bhakti tradition's view that wealth and liberation are not opposed but are both gifts of the same Divine Mother. (Sources: Sri Vidya Lakshmi tradition; Lakshmi Tantra commentaries.)
